fresh_facts.claim: US President Donald Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran on Sunday to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on key infrastructure including power plants and bridges.
US forces conducted a rescue operation for a missing F-15E crew member in Iran's mountains, using an abandoned airport in southern Isfahan province.
Iran claimed it foiled a US military rescue mission, stating it shot down two C-130 aircraft and destroyed several flying objects.
US and Israeli forces conducted strikes targeting Shahid Soleimani International Airport in southwestern Iran on Sunday.
Iran's central military command dismissed Trump's ultimatum and vowed to respond 'in kind', threatening to open the 'gates of hell' by striking US and Israeli infrastructure.
The IRGC claimed responsibility for drone attacks on two power and water desalination plants in Kuwait, causing significant damage and outages.
The IRGC claimed attacks on petrochemical plants in the UAE, Kuwait, Israel, and Bahrain, warning of intensified action against US economic interests.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died in a February 28 strike, with his son Mojtaba Khamenei appointed as successor.
Hardliner Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was named chief of the Supreme National Security Council following Ali Larijani's death in an Israeli strike.
US President Trump announced productive phone talks with Iran via envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff, with a 5-day postponement of strikes on Iranian energy sites.
Iran agreed to release 20 cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz as part of preliminary diplomatic discussions.
The US delivered a 15-point peace plan via Pakistan proposing Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Iran has no intent to negotiate.
Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied that talks with the US had occurred.
Trump warned of resuming bombings if diplomacy fails and eyed seizing enriched uranium or Kharg Island oil.
Mojtaba Khamenei made a rare statement vowing to remove adversaries' security.
New armed forces spokesperson General Abolfazl Shekarchi threatened global 'tourist centers'.
Egypt, Pakistan, and Turkey engaged as mediators between the US and Iran, with potential talks in Pakistan.
Over 2,000 additional US Marines are deploying before the diplomatic pause ends on Friday.
US Republican Representative Nancy Mace opposed the deployment of US troops to the region.
The ongoing conflict between the US-Israeli alliance and Iran has now lasted over five weeks.
Trump warned that the US 'hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran' and previewed attacks on bridges and power plants.
Fires occurred at UAE's Borouge Petrochemicals Factory from debris after air interceptions.
Iranian drones struck a government building in Kuwait City with no deaths reported.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 38 of conflict shows extreme polarization: simultaneous escalation with regional strikes on infrastructure and hostile military posturing, yet contradictory diplomatic signals with a 5-day pause, US 15-point peace plan, and mediation efforts, while Iranian leadership denies talks and threatens global targets. Hardliner Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr's appointment to security council signals IRGC dominance over diplomatic factions.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardliner, appointed chief of Supreme National Security Council on April 6, 2026, replacing Ali Larijani who died in Israeli strikes, solidifying IRGC control over Iranian military and security policy amid ongoing conflict.
